data.Specific Biochemistry & Approach: PSMA-targeting small molecule labeled with Actinium-225 alpha-emitter using the PSMA I&T ligand, which enables theranostic pairing with ^68^Ga-PSMA-I&T for imaging and demonstrates similar pharmacokinetics to ^177^Lu-PSMA-I&T [1]. The alpha-emitter delivers high linear energy transfer radiation (5.8-8.4 MeV per alpha particle) with short tissue penetration (47-85 µm) and high relative biological effectiveness compared to beta-emitters [1]. The therapy involves potential alpha-recoil effects where daughter nuclides (^221^Fr, ^213^Bi) may redistribute, contributing to off-target toxicity in kidneys and salivary glands [2]. The PSMA I&T ligand is used for both imaging and therapy, ensuring identical uptake patterns in tumors and healthy tissue [1].

data.Key Partnerships/Collaborators: Bayer acquired rights to actinium-225-based cancer compounds through the June 2021 acquisition of Noria Therapeutics Inc. and PSMA Therapeutics Inc. [3]. The acquired companies hold exclusive worldwide rights to technology licensed from Weill Cornell Medicine and Johns Hopkins University [3]. The 225Ac-PSMA I&T trial is an investigator-initiated phase I trial conducted at Erasmus MC in Rotterdam, The Netherlands, with collaboration from the European Commission's Joint Research Centre (JRC) in Karlsruhe, Germany [4].

data.Specific Biochemistry & Approach: Somatostatin receptor-targeting peptide (DOTAMTATE) complex radiolabeled with lead-212 (²¹²Pb), which serves as an in vivo generator of alpha particles for targeted alpha therapy [1]. The DOTAMTATE chelator binds to somatostatin receptor 2 (SSTR2) expressed on neuroendocrine tumor cells, delivering the alpha-emitting payload with high precision [2]. Lead-212 decays through a short-range alpha particle emission chain (via bismuth-212) that causes double-strand DNA breaks in cancer cells while minimizing damage to surrounding healthy tissue [2].

-  Phase 2 clinical trial completed [2]

Sanofi now leads global commercialization under September 2024 licensing agreement [1]. Abandonment signs: None; program has FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation, completed Phase 2, and secured major pharma partnership with Sanofi for commercialization.
data.Key Partnerships/Collaborators: Orano Med (212Pb supply and manufacturing through global industrial platform) [1]; Sanofi (exclusive licensing agreement for global commercialization, September 2024) [3]; RadioMedix (co-development partner) [1]. Orano Med maintains responsibility for manufacturing AlphaMedix through its integrated production platform [1] and has secured access to raw materials with 22,000 drums of purified thorium-232 for sustainable 212Pb production [4].

original_sources_full: [1] Press Release: Sanofi, RadioMedix, and Orano Med announce ...: "**Paris, France, and Houston, Texas, September 12, 2024. **As part of its effort to develop innovative treatments for people living with rare cancers, Sanofi has entered into an exclusive licensing agreement with RadioMedix, Inc., a US clinical-stage biotechnology company developing radiopharmaceuticals for PET imaging and targeted alpha therapy (TAT) against unmet medical needs in cancer, and Orano Med, a French clinical-stage biotechnology company, subsidiary of the Orano Group, developing lead-212 ( 212Pb) radioligand therapies (RLTs) against cancer. This collaboration between Sanofi, RadioMedix and Orano Med focuses specifically on the late-stage project, AlphaMedix TM ( 212Pb-DOTAMTATE), which currently is being evaluated for the treatment of adult patients with unresectable or metastatic, progressive somatostatin-receptor expressing neuroendocrine tumors (NETs), a rare cancer. AlphaMedix TM is a TAT which consists of a somatostatin receptor-targeting peptide complex radiolabeled with lead-212 ( 212Pb) that serves as an in vivo generator of alpha particles. ... TM, while Orano Med will be responsible for the manufacturing of AlphaMedix TM through its global industrial platform currently under development." (https://www.sanofi.com/en/media-room/press-releases/2024/2024-09-12-05-00-00-2944919, Conf=0.85)

[2] AlphaMedix™ Shows Promising Phase 2 Results in Advanced ...: "AlphaMedix is a targeted alpha emitter that delivers a highly potent radioactive isotope, lead-212 (212Pb), directly to tumor cells expressing somatostatin receptors, which are commonly found on NET cells. ... In the current Phase 2 study, AlphaMedix led to measurable tumor responses and met predefined safety criteria." (https://netrf.org/2025/10/17/alphamedix-phase-2/, Conf=0.85)
[3] Pipeline - Orano Med: "**peptide radiolabeled with**for the treatment of somatostatin receptor expressing neuroendocrine tumors (NETs)." (https://www.oranomed.com/en/pipeline, Conf=0.85)

[4] Industrial platform: "**22,000 drums of purified thorium-232**at its disposal, a **sufficiently plentiful stock of raw materials to cover global needs for Targeted Alpha Therapy.**" (https://www.oranomed.com/en/industrial-platform, Conf=0.95)
